content.problemStatement: Write a C program that takes two floating-point numbers as input and calculates their product. Print the result with two decimal places.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two floating-point numbers num1 and num2 separated by a single space.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a line in the format "Product: X" where X is the product of num1 and num2 rounded to exactly two decimal places.

solution.approach: The problem only requires multiplying two numbers for each test case and printing the result with two decimal places. No special algorithm is needed – just read the inputs, compute a*b using double precision, and format the output using standard I/O manipulators (printf, cout with fixed<<setprecision, String.format, f-strings, toFixed). The only subtlety is correct rounding to two decimal places, which the standard library functions handle correctly. The overall complexity is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one multiplication per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used irrespective of T.

code.Python3:
import sys
from decimal import Decimal, ROUND_HALF_UP, getcontext

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    T = int(data[0])
    out = []
    idx = 1
    getcontext().prec = 28
    for _ in range(T):
        a_str = data[idx]
        b_str = data[idx+1]
        idx += 2
        a = Decimal(a_str)
        b = Decimal(b_str)
        prod = (a * b).quantize(Decimal('0.01'), rounding=ROUND_HALF_UP)
        out.append(f"Product: {prod:.2f}")
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
